chief_complaint: A 35-year-old man with poorly controlled diabetes mellitus and a strong family history of diabetes.
history: The patient was diagnosed with type 1 diabetes mellitus at the age of 16 after hospitalization due to diabetic ketoacidosis and positive glutamic acid decarboxylase (GAD) antibodies. He has a strong family history of diabetes and prediabetes. Genetic testing was conducted for monogenic diabetes, which revealed a heterozygote Glucokinase (GCK) gene mutation, indicative of maturity-onset diabetes of the young (MODY) type 2.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are provided.
labs: Positive glutamic acid decarboxylase (GAD) antibodies, heterozygote Glucokinase (GCK) gene mutation on exome panel.
correct_diagnosis: Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus with Monogenic Diabetes (MODY 2)
differentials: Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus with Monogenic Diabetes (MODY 2)
correct_ranking: Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus with Monogenic Diabetes (MODY 2)
